docstring:
/**
 * @brief Writes the object's stored message to std::cout, followed by a newline
 *        flush, and increments the invocation counter.
 *
 * Emits exactly @c this->length bytes from @c this->message via
 * @c std::cout.write, then inserts @c std::endl (which outputs '\n' and
 * flushes the stream). After the write, @c this->call_count is incremented
 * by one to track how many times this method has been invoked on the
 * instance.
 *
 * @note The compiler inlined the string insertion as a direct
 *       @c std::cout.write(message, length) call, and @c std::endl was
 *       expanded to the fast path that consults
 *       @c std::ctype<char>::_M_table[0x38] before either putting '\n'
 *       directly or falling back to the virtual @c do_widen path.
 * @note No bounds checking is performed; the caller must ensure
 *       @c message points to at least @c length valid bytes.
 * @note This member mutates @c call_count, so it is not @c const.
 */
assumptions: Symbol is a C++ mangled name (_ZN10HelloWorld12printMessageEv); emitted C++ despite the requested 'c' target because a C rewrite would misrepresent the code.

Collapsed the cout.write + ctype-facet-flag fast-path + put('\n') + flush sequence into the idiomatic `std::cout << std::endl;`. The vtable-slot-0x30 vs length comparison guarding do_widen is not reproduced; assumed equivalent to std::endl for the default locale.
Struct layout for HelloWorld inferred from field accesses: const char* message @0, size_t length @8, int call_count @0x20. Other members not recovered; padding between length and call_count omitted.
call_count member name is inferred from the `add [this+0x20], 1` behavior; true name unknown. Width assumed int.
length typed as size_t; cast to std::streamsize for write().

Second code block starting at L_1a4b (its own prologue, operates on a vector-of-string-like container, calls sub_12b0 and per-element free via 0x1260) treated as a separate function appended to the disassembly and excluded. If it is actually part of printMessage, destructor/deallocation side effects are dropped.

Dropped optimizer artefacts: this-pointer spill to var1, TLS/vtable chain reload `*(rbp-0x18)` for ctype facet lookup, and the movsx sign-extension of the widened character — all folded into std::endl.
Renamings (obj/expected/pair/offset/tmp/flags) described the endl-inlining scratch values and were elided rather than surfaced as named locals.
divergences: [high] dropped_call: The entire second half of the function (L_1a4b onward) — which calls 0x12b0(), iterates a container [rbp..var1] in steps of 32 bytes calling 0x1260() on each element whose inline-buffer pointer differs from stack_top, and conditionally frees the container's backing storage via a tail-call to 0x1260 — is excluded from the rewrite. The rewriter justifies this by assuming it's a separate function, but the original pseudocode presents it as a single fn with fall-through control flow; if it's actually part of printMessage these destructor/deallocation calls (and their side effects on the heap) are silently dropped.

[medium] control_flow: Original has an explicit branch: if ctype facet pointer (*(rbp+ret+0xf0)) is null, jump to L_1a4b (the dropped block) instead of doing the endl sequence. The rewrite unconditionally emits `<< std::endl`, losing that null-facet fallback path entirely.

[medium] dropped_call: Original calls 0x1280 with (cout, message, length) which is most likely `std::__ostream_insert` or `operator<<(ostream&, const string&)`, not `ostream::write`. Using write() bypasses the sentry object, width/fill formatting, and exception/badbit handling that the real operator<< performs.

[low] changed_width: Width of the counter at +0x20 is inferred as int from `add [this+0x20], 1`; the original increments a full word (could be int32 or int64 depending on actual instruction encoding which isn't shown). Name is also a guess.

[low] other: Padding between length (+0x08) and call_count (+0x20) is omitted; unrecovered members acknowledged by rewriter. Does not affect the emitted code but means the struct is not a faithful layout.

[low] dropped_side_effect: The sign-extension (movsx) of the widened character and the TLS/vtable chain reload are folded away; acceptable if std::endl substitution is valid, but the exact sequence of virtual calls to ctype::do_widen is no longer visible.
